{{nihongo|'''Asori Shrine (Nakami, Meiwa Town)'''|麻續神社（明和町中海）}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Asori Shrine (Nakami, Meiwa Town)'''|麻續神社（明和町中海）}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Asori Shrine (Nakami, Meiwa Town)'''|麻續神社（明和町中海）}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Asori Shrine (Nakami, Meiwa Town)'''|麻續神社（明和町中海）}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Asori Shrine (Nakami, Meiwa Town)'''|麻續神社（明和町中海）}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
| 名称 = 麻續神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=多気郡}}
| 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海87
| 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
| 祭神 = {{jalink|豊城入彦命|Q11633375}}
| 神体 = 
| 社格 =  {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
| 創建 = 
| 本殿 = 
| 別名 = 
| 札所等 = 
| 例祭 = 11月3日
| 神事 = 
| 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=麻續神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''麻續神社'''（おおみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、不詳であるが{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}の式内社に比定されている。{{jalink|斎宮跡|Q3135853}}の北に広がる田園地帯に鎮座しており、近くを歴代{{jalink|斎王|Q19841349}}の禊の場である{{jalink|祓川 (三重県)|Q11588499|祓川}}が流れる。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}45年、村内の6社が合祀されその際に麻續神社と称した<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 麻續神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
*主祭神：{{jalink|豊城入彦命|Q11633375}}
*配祀神：{{jalink|豊鍬入姫命|Q10532771}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|宇迦之御魂神}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 麻續神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：11月3日
*祈年祭：2月18日
*新嘗祭：11月23日

=== 交通 ===
* {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}、「{{jalink|斎宮駅|Q7400129}}」下車、北へ徒歩約30分

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== 関連項目 ===
* {{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}

{{デフォルトソート:おおみしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''麻續神社'''（おおみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、不詳であるが{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}の式内社に比定されている。{{jalink|斎宮跡|Q3135853}}の北に広がる田園地帯に鎮座しており、近くを歴代{{jalink|斎王|Q19841349}}の禊の場である{{jalink|祓川 (三重県)|Q11588499|祓川}}が流れる。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}45年、村内の6社が合祀されその際に麻續神社と称した<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 麻續神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
*主祭神：{{jalink|豊城入彦命|Q11633375}}
*配祀神：{{jalink|豊鍬入姫命|Q10532771}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|宇迦之御魂神}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 麻續神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：11月3日
*祈年祭：2月18日
*新嘗祭：11月23日

=== 交通 ===
* {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}、「{{jalink|斎宮駅|Q7400129}}」下車、北へ徒歩約30分

'''麻續神社'''（おおみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、不詳であるが{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}の式内社に比定されている。{{jalink|斎宮跡|Q3135853}}の北に広がる田園地帯に鎮座しており、近くを歴代{{jalink|斎王|Q19841349}}の禊の場である{{jalink|祓川 (三重県)|Q11588499|祓川}}が流れる。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}45年、村内の6社が合祀されその際に麻續神社と称した<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 麻續神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
*主祭神：{{jalink|豊城入彦命|Q11633375}}
*配祀神：{{jalink|豊鍬入姫命|Q10532771}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|宇迦之御魂神}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 麻續神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：11月3日
*祈年祭：2月18日
*新嘗祭：11月23日

=== 交通 ===
* {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}、「{{jalink|斎宮駅|Q7400129}}」下車、北へ徒歩約30分

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== 関連項目 ===
* {{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}

{{デフォルトソート:おおみしんしや}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''麻續神社'''（おおみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、不詳であるが{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}の式内社に比定されている。{{jalink|斎宮跡|Q3135853}}の北に広がる田園地帯に鎮座しており、近くを歴代{{jalink|斎王|Q19841349}}の禊の場である{{jalink|祓川 (三重県)|Q11588499|祓川}}が流れる。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}45年、村内の6社が合祀されその際に麻續神社と称した<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 麻續神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
*主祭神：{{jalink|豊城入彦命|Q11633375}}
*配祀神：{{jalink|豊鍬入姫命|Q10532771}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|宇迦之御魂神}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 麻續神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：11月3日
*祈年祭：2月18日
*新嘗祭：11月23日

=== 交通 ===
* {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}、「{{jalink|斎宮駅|Q7400129}}」下車、北へ徒歩約30分

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== 関連項目 ===
* {{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{jalink|Saio|Q19841349}}. In the 45th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: Get off at {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Engishiki shrines in Ise Province|Q11378973}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Omi Shrine}}

'''麻續神社'''（おおみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、不詳であるが{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}の式内社に比定されている。{{jalink|斎宮跡|Q3135853}}の北に広がる田園地帯に鎮座しており、近くを歴代{{jalink|斎王|Q19841349}}の禊の場である{{jalink|祓川 (三重県)|Q11588499|祓川}}が流れる。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}45年、村内の6社が合祀されその際に麻續神社と称した<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 麻續神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
*主祭神：{{jalink|豊城入彦命|Q11633375}}
*配祀神：{{jalink|豊鍬入姫命|Q10532771}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|宇迦之御魂神}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 麻續神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：11月3日
*祈年祭：2月18日
*新嘗祭：11月23日

=== 交通 ===
* {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}、「{{jalink|斎宮駅|Q7400129}}」下車、北へ徒歩約30分

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{jalink|Saio|Q19841349}}. In the 45th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: Get off at {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

== Japanese content ==

| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=多気郡}}
| 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海87
| 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}

| 神体 =

| 創建 =

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''麻續神社'''（おおみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|明和町 (三重県)|Q1203258|明和町}}大字中海にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、不詳であるが{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}の式内社に比定されている。{{jalink|斎宮跡|Q3135853}}の北に広がる田園地帯に鎮座しており、近くを歴代{{jalink|斎王|Q19841349}}の禊の場である{{jalink|祓川 (三重県)|Q11588499|祓川}}が流れる。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}45年、村内の6社が合祀されその際に麻續神社と称した<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 麻續神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
*主祭神：{{jalink|豊城入彦命|Q11633375}}
*配祀神：{{jalink|豊鍬入姫命|Q10532771}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|宇迦之御魂神}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 麻續神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：11月3日
*祈年祭：2月18日
*新嘗祭：11月23日

=== 交通 ===
* {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}、「{{jalink|斎宮駅|Q7400129}}」下車、北へ徒歩約30分

=== 脚注 ===

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}{{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū Site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{jalink|Saiō|Q19841349}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: From {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}{{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū Site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{jalink|Saiō|Q19841349}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: From {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}{{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū Site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{jalink|Saiō|Q19841349}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: From {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Engishiki shrines of Ise Province|Q11378973}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Omi Shrine}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{jalink|Saio|Q19841349}}. In the 45th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: Get off at {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}{{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū Site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{jalink|Saiō|Q19841349}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: From {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Engishiki shrines of Ise Province|Q11378973}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Omi Shrine}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{jalink|Saio|Q19841349}}. In the 45th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: Get off at {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{jalink|Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|Meiwa Town}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}}; its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}{{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{jalink|Saikū Site|Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{jalink|Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{jalink|Saiō|Q19841349}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{jalink|Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{jalink|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771}}; {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}; {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{jalink|Toyouke-hime||Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}}: From {{jalink|Saikū Station|Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

== Japanese content ==

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill||lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill||lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill||lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}; {{ill||lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime||WD=|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}

=== See also ===

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime||WD=|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine

| Image = {{Image requested|cat=Taki District}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime||WD=|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Ōmi Shrine'''|麻續神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===
* {{ill|lt=List of Engishiki shrines of Ise Province|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{ill|lt=Saio|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In the 45th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{ill|lt=Saio|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349}}. In the 45th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

=== Notes ===

{{Reflist|2}}

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, about a 30-minute walk north

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{ill|lt=Saio|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In the 45th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, about a 30-minute walk north

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{ill|lt=Saio|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In the 45th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, about a 30-minute walk north

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{ill|lt=Saio|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In the 45th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, about a 30-minute walk north

== Japanese content ==

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa River (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{ill|lt=Saio|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In the 45th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.

'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa, Mie|Q1203258|WD=Q1203258|lt=Meiwa Town|1=Meiwa, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Q3135853|WD=Q3135853|1=Ruins of Saikū}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa (Mie Prefecture)|Q11588499|WD=Q11588499|lt=Haraigawa|1=Harai River}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Q19841349|WD=Q19841349|1=Saiō}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Q10532771|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Q7400129|WD=Q7400129|1=Saikū Station}}, about a 30-minute walk north
