'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa Town|Meiwa, Mie|WD=Q1203258}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of founding is unknown, but it is identified as one of the Engishiki-listed shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Ise Province|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Taki District|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū Site|Ruins of Saikū|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa|Harai River|WD=Q11588499}}, which served as a place of purification for successive {{ill|lt=Saiō|Saiō|WD=Q19841349}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} 45 (1912), six shrines within the village were enshrined together, at which time it came to be called Omi Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|Toyosukiiri-hime|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime no Kami|UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinen-sai (prayer for a good harvest): February 18
* Niiname-sai (harvest festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085}}: From {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Saikū Station|WD=Q7400129}}, about a 30-minute walk north

=== Notes ===
{{Footnote help}}

{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===
* {{ill|lt=List of Engishiki shrines of Ise Province|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|WD=Q11378973}}


'''Omi Shrine''' (おおみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōaza Nakami, {{ill|lt=Meiwa Town|Meiwa, Mie|WD=Q1203258}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
The date of establishment is unknown, but it is identified as a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Ise Province|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Taki District|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360}}. The shrine stands in a rural area spreading north of the {{ill|lt=Saikū site|Ruins of Saikū|WD=Q3135853}}, and nearby flows the {{ill|lt=Haraigawa|Harai River|WD=Q11588499}}, which served as the purification site for successive {{ill|lt=Saio|Saiō|WD=Q19841349}}. In the 45th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} (1912), six shrines within the village were merged, and at that time it came to be called Omi Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e9%ba%bb%e7%ba%8c%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Toyokiiri-hiko no Mikoto|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375}}
* Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Toyokuninushi-hime no Mikoto|Toyosukiiri-hime|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}; {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines – Omi Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63378|access-date=2025-12-28}}</ref>

=== Festivals and rituals ===
* Annual festival: November 3
* Kinensai (Prayer for Harvest): February 18
* Niinamesai (Harvest Festival): November 23

=== Access ===
* {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=Saikū Station|Saikū Station|WD=Q7400129}}, then walk north for about 30 minutes.
